div.h1: Phase 3: Recursive Chunk Deallocation (The Critical Loop)
div.p: MemoryPool.t_cleanup: { # Reclaim Bitmap MemoryPool -> SystemHeap: "free(pool->bitmap)" SystemHeap --> MemoryPool: "Bitmap Array [0x%p] Released"

pre.code: # Mutex Teardown MemoryPool -> MemoryPool: "pthread_mutex_unlock(&pool->lock)" MemoryPool -> SystemHeap: "pthread_mutex_destroy(&pool->lock)" SystemHeap --> MemoryPool: "OS Sync Object [0x%p] Released"

div.h1: Phase 5: Final Memory Sanitization & Valgrind Hooks
div.p:
MemoryPool.safety_note: "CRITICAL SECTION:
Mutex must encompass
full chunk walk to avoid
UAF if threads try to
release_block during destruction."

SystemHeap.note: "OS ALLOCATOR:
Memory may remain resident
in process RSS. Use
malloc_trim(0) to force
release to kernel." }
div.h1: Summary Table (Positioned relative to the sequence container)

tr.td: POOL_TERMINATING (0x03) set prior to list traversal.
Blocking mutex lock. Re-entry results in EAGAIN or early exit.
memset(pool, 0, ...) triggers immediate SIGSEGV on invalid access.
Aggressive warning log; forced reclamation of all tracked chunks.
